channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Open-top bin, 8th April
item.description:
We live opposite the green space on Belvedere road and note a workman often attends to litter pick from the area surrounding the bin, however ultimately the problem persists and the litter in the bin is reachable by animals (I assume foxes or magpies), with the contents being carried out of the bin and onto the neighbouring green area. It's a mild frustration and eye sore, but I just wondered if a closed top bin could be installed to replace the existing bin and hopefully remove the issue altogether, while saving labour hours for the street cleansing team! Appreciate the consideration.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Belvedere Road, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overflowing black bins - please collect, 8th April
item.description:
Bins

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 43, Ronver Road, Catford South, Grove Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Trees damaged, 8th April
item.description:
Tree on communal garden at the back of St Marks House, Queens Row side. Possibly vandalism and at the front of St Marks House the of the post of a new tree the string is broken and need securing. Its in corner of communal Garden near the bloke and fence.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Phelp Street, Walworth, Greater London

item.title: Graffiti on Sundridge Avenue Comms Box, 8th April
item.description:
Unfortunately the same scribble has been graffitied again. Please can you remove.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Sundridge Avenue, Elmstead, Greater London

item.title: Electrical cupboard left open with exposed wires, 8th April
item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 32, Heaton Road, Peckham, Greater London

item.title: 143-151 High St Penge, bare plywood builders' hoardings securing the fire damaged Poundland store - about 4 Sq m of red outline spray paint tags, 8th April
item.description:
143-151 High St Penge, bare plywood builders' hoardings securing the fire damaged Poundland store - about 4 Sq m of red outline spray paint tags This is compounding the detrimental visual impact of the fire damaged shop as a place of crime and lawlessness.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 143-151, High Street, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fly-tipping of waste by neighbour in skip, 8th April
item.description:
Household and electrical waste has been dumped in our skip on two nights running. The first night the person was caught by another neighbour filling their skip at no. 4 Lubbock Road and was told to remove it, around 7pm. They subsequently took some of the items to my skip on Porrington Close. They returned this evening sometime between 6.20pm and 8.20pm to dump more waste. Some of the items will have to be removed from the skip for special disposal such as electrical items, 5 ft LED tube lights, a tattoo gun and inks. Some of the packaging has the owner's name and address on. They live in Lower Camden. I can no longer use the skip as it has been filled with this person's waste, and the skip company will refuse to take it if I don't remove the prohibited items.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Porrington Close, Elmstead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: South Aldershot, 8th April
item.description:
Boxes

item.category: Fly-tip Small - Less than one bag

item.title: Wheelie bin unsanitary, 8th April
item.description:
The wrong items have been placed into this bin and so it has not been collected. This bin is in a communal space and has not been collected for some time and has become unsanitary attracting rats. Please can it be emptied?

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Merryfield, Blackheath
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Suspecylted illegally parked vehicle. Not recognised as a resident., 8th April
item.description:
Car parked an hour. Not recognised as parking here. Occupants not recognised as residents. When leaving the vehicle the passenger threw rubbish including a plastic bottle onto the grass.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Birchmore Walk, Highbury, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street lights outside St John's, Waterloo not working, 8th April
item.description:

item.title: Dumping rubbish in the alleyway, 8th April
item.description:
Regarding dumping of rubbish in the alleyway near the sub station box near samson house maxey road i have cctv footage of what house they went in as well
